objective: Domain expansion feasibility study for PaperSift entity-based clustering

domains.name: Drug Discovery / QSAR
challenges: Low entity density: mean 1.1 entities/title vs ~2.1 for virtual cell; 30% of titles get zero entity hits with current vocabulary
Highly abbreviation-heavy domain: QSAR, QSPR, IC50, EC50, ADMET appear in titles as acronyms requiring exact-match patterns
Broad sub-field heterogeneity: computational chemistry, toxicology, cheminformatics, environmental QSAR, antimicrobial peptides, materials QSAR all coexist

Overlap with existing vocabulary is moderate (14/80 terms = 17.5%): ML method terms (deep learning, random forest) are shared but domain-specific terms (molecular docking, pharmacophore, ADMET) are absent
~30% of papers use generic ML vocabulary that will link QSAR papers to unrelated computational biology papers
ChEMBL, DrugBank, PubChem already in existing entity list — risk of cross-domain pollution if used with virtual cell papers
Vocabulary requires significant expansion: docking, binding affinity, scaffold, IC50, ADMET, Lipinski, fingerprint must all be added as explicit patterns
domains.feasibility_rationale: The domain has a large paper pool (87K papers) and distinct vocabulary. However, entity density is low (1.1/title), 30% titles get zero hits, and extensive new entity patterns are required. With vocabulary extension of ~50 new terms, clustering is viable but will require tuning. Recommend pilot on 500-paper title-search subset first.

domains.name: Gut Microbiome-Disease
challenges: Severe entity concentration: 'microbiome' hits 54% of titles and 'gut microbiome' hits 48% — these become universal connectors causing all papers to cluster together
Must treat 'microbiome', 'microbiota', 'gut microbiome', 'gut microbiota' as STOPWORDS (too ubiquitous) and rely on disease + method terms for discrimination
Low overlap with existing vocabulary (4/90 = 4.4%): nearly all vocabulary must be added from scratch
Disease terms are highly heterogeneous: IBD, Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, diabetes, obesity, colorectal cancer, liver cirrhosis — each a sub-cluster anchor
Taxonomic terms (Firmicutes, Bacteroidetes, Lactobacillus) appear mostly in sub-field papers, not review/overview titles — low title-level frequency
Method terms (16S rRNA, metagenomics, UniFrac, QIIME) are methodological sub-clusters that may fragment disease-focused clustering
Large paper pool (159K) requires aggressive title-search filtering or concept-level pre-filtering to get tractable dataset
domains.feasibility_rationale: High entity density (2.2/title, similar to virtual cell's ~2.1) and 96.4% title coverage. The domain naturally partitions into disease axes (IBD, neurological, metabolic, oncological), method axes (16S rRNA, metagenomics, FMT), and taxon axes (specific bacteria). Main risk: 'microbiome'/'microbiota' must be downweighted or excluded as stopwords. With ~40 new entity patterns added, PaperSift clustering should produce 8-15 meaningful clusters on a 2,000-5,000 paper dataset.

comparative_summary.rationale: Gut microbiome has higher entity density, better title coverage (96.4% vs 70.5%), and naturally distinct disease/method/taxon sub-clusters well-suited to Leiden clustering. QSAR requires more vocabulary work and has fundamental entity sparsity issues.
comparative_summary.shared_risk: Both domains require substantial new entity vocabulary (~40-50 terms) not in the current 224-term list
quick_wins: Gut microbiome: add dysbiosis, IBD, butyrate, SCFAs, Firmicutes, Bacteroidetes, FMT, 16S rRNA as stopword-excluded anchors
QSAR: add molecular docking, binding affinity, ADMET, IC50, pharmacophore, scaffold — already has deep learning / random forest via existing list

methodology_notes.entity_analysis_basis: Top 56-61 cited papers from OpenAlex title search
methodology_notes.overlap_method: case-insensitive exact string match against existing entity_layer.py vocabulary
methodology_notes.specificity_threshold: 5-60% title hit rate = ideal discriminating entity
